era: 1st Century AD, Roman-occupied Judea and Galilee

visual_dna.skin_tone: warm olive-brown, sun-weathered from years of outdoor ministry
visual_dna.beard_style: full beard, medium length, natural — not groomed
visual_dna.eye_color: dark brown — deep, perceptive, others notice them
visual_dna.height_build: average for era (~5'5"), lean but strong from carpentry and walking hundreds of miles
distinguishing_marks: calm bearing that reads as authority, not aggression

wardrobe_by_arc.ministry: simple undyed linen tunic, outer robe (tallith with tassels/tzitzit), worn leather sandals
wardrobe_by_arc.transfiguration: radiant white garments — description: 'his clothes became as bright as a flash of lightning'
wardrobe_by_arc.passion: worn tunic, crown of thorns, purple robe (mockery), later stripped
wardrobe_by_arc.post_resurrection: clean linen, unrecognized at first — ordinary yet extraordinary

voice_profile.register: warm baritone — not thunderous, authority through depth not volume
voice_profile.pace: measured, never hurried — comfortable with silence before key statements
voice_profile.accent_note: Galilean Aramaic speaker — his accent was noted and identifiable (Mark 14:70)
speech_mannerisms: uses parable and story naturally, not as technique
quotes Torah with authority — 'you have heard it said... but I tell you'
addresses individuals by name at pivotal moments (Zacchaeus, Simon, Martha)
long pauses before the most important words
voice_profile.notes: Never raises his voice in anger except once — the Temple. In every other context, his authority is quiet.

emotional_arc.opening: Joy and purpose at launch of ministry — reading Isaiah in Nazareth synagogue: 'Today this scripture is fulfilled'
emotional_arc.midpoint: Growing tension — setting his face toward Jerusalem (Luke 9:51), knowing what awaits. Teaching with increasing urgency.
emotional_arc.climax: Gethsemane — 'Father, if you are willing, take this cup from me. Yet not my will, but yours.' Sweat like drops of blood. Then the cross.
emotional_arc.resolution: Resurrection appearances — calm, unhurried, eating fish with them. 'Peace be with you.' The wound marks still present.

evangelical_baptist.notes: The fulfillment of prophecy, substitutionary atonement, resurrection — every core doctrine in Luke
evangelical_baptist.marketing_angle: The fulfillment of every prophecy, walking among us — not a story. History.

catholic.notes: The Incarnate Word, Real Presence, institution of Eucharist at Last Supper
catholic.marketing_angle: The Incarnate Word — fully human at the table, fully divine at the tomb

black_church.notes: Liberation theology gold — Jesus centers the poor, the outcast, the oppressed throughout Luke
black_church.marketing_angle: The liberator who walked into rooms with broken people and made them feel seen

hispanic_christian.notes: El milagro, la familia, el sufrimiento — suffering and resurrection resonate deeply
hispanic_christian.marketing_angle: El Salvador del mundo — healer, miracle-worker, the one who never gives up on family

secular_curious.notes: The historical Jesus — most compelling figure in human history. Luke written as historical account.
secular_curious.marketing_angle: The most compelling, disruptive figure who ever lived — who was he really?

youth_gen_z.notes: Rejected by institutions, radical in love, died for what he believed, changed everything
youth_gen_z.marketing_angle: Rejected by the system. Died for what he believed. Changed everything anyway.

jewish_american.notes: Portrayed as deeply Jewish — the Torah-observant rabbi from Galilee. Not the European figure.
jewish_american.marketing_angle: A Jewish rabbi from Galilee — his story told in its actual historical context

women.notes: Luke centers women more than any other gospel — Mary, Elizabeth, the women who funded his ministry, Mary Magdalene
women.marketing_angle: He noticed women everyone else overlooked. In Luke, they're never the background.

men.notes: Strong, confrontational, courageous — overturns tables, faces death without flinching. Not passive.
men.marketing_angle: Fearless. Direct. Walked toward danger. The toughest thing he ever said was 'forgive them.'

audience_targeting.modern_parallel: The leader who walks into rooms with broken people and makes them feel seen
audience_targeting.modern_parallel_detail: Not a CEO, not a general — but someone with genuine authority that doesn't need a title. People left everything to follow him.

ai_generation.consistency_seed_prompt: 1st century Jewish man age 30-33, warm olive-brown sun-weathered skin, dark wavy shoulder-length hair, full natural beard, dark brown eyes with depth and calm authority, lean strong build from years of walking, simple undyed linen tunic and outer robe with tassels, worn leather sandals, cinematic photoreal, golden hour natural light, 8k, film grain
ai_generation.negative_prompt: blue eyes, blonde hair, European features, white skin, halo, glowing body, cartoonish, painted illustration, modern clothing, medieval costume, muscular bodybuilder physique, long flowing luxurious hair
ai_generation.midjourney_style_params: cinematic photoreal, golden hour natural light, shallow depth of field, film grain
ai_generation.firefly_prompt: Photorealistic portrait of a 1st century Jewish man, age 30-33, olive-brown sun-weathered complexion, dark wavy shoulder-length hair, full natural beard, deep dark brown eyes radiating calm authority, lean build, wearing simple linen robes and sandals, Galilean hillside background, warm golden hour light, shallow depth of field, cinematic drama
reference_films: The Passion of the Christ (2004) — texture, grit, physicality of suffering
Son of God (2014) — warmth and approachability of ministry scenes
ai_generation.color_grading_notes: Warm golden tones for ministry. Cool blue-grey for passion scenes. Dawn light for resurrection.
